pom.xml.general_idea: Migrate javax.annotation-api dependency to jakarta.annotation-api to align with Jakarta EE namespace changes in newer JDKs.
pom.xml.why_exists: javax.annotation-api is deprecated/removed in JDK 21+ environments; code relying on javax.annotation will fail to compile or link if the artifact is not present or if the namespace is strictly enforced.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update maven-compiler-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin may not support compiling against JDK 21 or may have bugs with newer language features.

wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 1.8 to 21.
wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile against Java 8 semantics, failing to utilize or enforce Java 21 features and potentially causing runtime incompatibilities if Java 21-specific APIs are used.

wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and modern Java compilation.
wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the maven-compiler-plugin may not fully support or correctly handle the 'release' flag for Java 21, or may lack necessary bug fixes for newer JDKs.

wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated 'source' and 'target' configuration with the modern 'release' flag for cross-compilation.
wire-di-bootstrap/pom.xml.why_exists: Using 'source' and 'target' separately is deprecated in favor of 'release' for Java 9+; while it might still compile, it is not the correct way to target Java 21 and can lead to subtle class file version mismatches.
